description: Explains how pytest turns command-line inputs into a collection tree, how collection nodes are constructed and identified, and how filesystem and Python collectors produce runnable test items.
document:
pytest collection is the phase that turns command-line inputs and configured discovery rules into a tree of collectors and runnable test items. The collection options in src/_pytest/main.py control the main user-facing choices: collecting without running, treating arguments as Python packages, ignoring paths or glob patterns, deselecting by node id prefix, keeping duplicate inputs, recursing into virtual environments, continuing after collection errors, and choosing how test modules are imported. The user documentation in goodpractices.rst and pythoncollection.rst describes the same model from the outside: pytest starts from configured test paths or command-line arguments, recurses through directories unless excluded, finds Python test modules by configured file patterns, and then looks for test functions, methods, and classes by naming conventions.

`Session` is the root collector for a run. It is created from the configuration with the root path as its own path and an empty node id, then it records collection state such as initial arguments, cached collection reports, collected items, and failure counters. Its collection entry point resolves each argument into a path plus any selected names after separators, honors package-style arguments when requested, and normalizes overlapping or duplicate inputs unless duplicate preservation is enabled. It also remembers the explicit initial paths and their parents so that direct command-line targets are not accidentally ignored by normal recursion filters. After collecting the root, it expands collectors into items unless the caller asked for unexpanded collectors, applies deselection and plugin item modification hooks, and records the final item count.

The base model in src/_pytest/nodes.py is intentionally small. `Node` stores the local name, parent, inherited config and session, filesystem path, markers, keyword state, plugin stash, and the stable node id used in reporting and selection. Direct construction is blocked by its metaclass; plugins and subclasses are expected to use `from_parent`, which prevents callers from manually passing config or session and keeps those values inherited from the tree. `Collector` represents internal tree nodes that can produce children, while `Item` represents a runnable leaf. Node helpers walk the parent chain, find the closest parent of a type, add or iterate markers, register finalizers through the session setup state, and emit warnings at the node's source location.

Filesystem collectors adapt that node model to paths. The shared filesystem collector computes a name from the path relative to its parent when possible and computes a node id relative to the root path or an explicitly requested initial path, normalizing separators for stable ids. The default directory collector in main.py scans entries, asks path-sensitive hooks whether to ignore each directory or file, and yields directory or file collectors from the hook system. The Python plugin supplies the package-aware branch: directories with an initializer become `Package` collectors, while ordinary directories stay as `Dir` collectors. Both scan children similarly, but the package collector imports and handles package-level setup before collecting children, and it orders the initializer before other entries.

Python collection in src/_pytest/python.py converts eligible files and objects into this tree. A Python file becomes a `Module` collector only if it is an explicit initial path or matches the configured Python file patterns. Importing is delegated to `import_path`, which supports the traditional path-prepending and path-appending modes as well as an importlib mode that avoids modifying sys.path and allows same-named test modules in different locations. Within a module or class, the collector inspects definition-ordered attributes, skips opt-out objects, optionally avoids tests imported from other modules, and calls the plugin hook that creates class collectors or function items. Parametrized functions produce multiple item names with parameter ids, and a class item gets a fresh instance per function. Tests in testing/test_collection.py exercise this addressing model by collecting a single function, a class method, parametrized variants, custom file nodes, and package-contained modules by their node ids.
